title: Traditional flapping bird
materials: origami-washi (15cm square, or cartridge-paper-90 cut square)
sources: Traditional Japanese flapping bird model, public domain. Documented in late-Meiji kindergarten origami primers (pre-1928) via Internet Archive origami history collection. The model predates any named modern designer.
notes: The flapping bird: hold the tail and neck, pull gently outward, the wings flap. A classic kinetic origami model. Uses only valley and mountain folds (no inside reverse, no petal fold required). The base is the preliminary base (square base). Folds from the preliminary base: kite fold on all four flaps, then narrow the model by folding the flaps in to the centreline on all four sides, fold the bottom point up to become the tail and the upper point up to become the head, spread the wings. The wing flap mechanic: the two lower points (tail and head) are pulled gently outward, and the wings open. Covers the fold sequence with origamiFoldDiagram.
